title: Suspicious text claiming to be from Chase Bank
description: Received a text message saying my Chase account has been locked and I need to click a link to verify my identity. The link goes to a domain that is not chase.com. The message came from an unknown number and used urgent language.
summary: This report describes a possible phishing attempt designed to steal credentials or personal information. Exercise caution and avoid interacting with the suspicious message.
checklist: Do not click any links or download attachments from the suspicious message.
If you already clicked, change your password immediately for the affected account.
Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on your accounts.
Report the phishing attempt to the impersonated organization via their official website.

title: IRS phone scam demanding gift cards
description: Someone called claiming to be from the IRS saying I owe back taxes and must pay immediately with iTunes gift cards or face arrest. They were very aggressive and said police were on the way. This is clearly a scam but I wanted to report it for others.
summary: This report describes a potential scam or fraud attempt seeking money or sensitive information. Do not engage with the requester.
checklist: Stop all communication with the suspected scammer immediately.
Do not send money, gift cards, or personal information.
Report the scam to the FTC at reportfraud.ftc.gov.
Alert your bank or financial institution if you shared payment details.

title: Neighbor's Wi-Fi network compromised
description: My neighbor noticed unknown devices connected to their home router. Their wifi password was changed without their knowledge, and they could not access the router admin panel. They suspect someone hacked into their network remotely.
summary: This report describes a potential network or cybersecurity threat. Investigate affected systems and secure network access promptly.
checklist: Disconnect the affected device from the network if you suspect active compromise.
Change your Wi-Fi password and any reused passwords immediately.
Run a full antivirus/malware scan on all connected devices.
Check your router firmware for updates and disable remote management.

title: Unauthorized credit card opened in my name
description: I received a letter congratulating me on a new credit card that I never applied for. Someone appears to have used my social security number to open this account. I have placed a freeze on my credit report but wanted to alert the community.
summary: This report indicates possible identity theft or unauthorized use of personal information. Take immediate steps to secure your accounts and credit.
checklist: Place a fraud alert or credit freeze with all three credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion).
Review your credit report for any unauthorized accounts or inquiries.
File an identity theft report at identitytheft.gov.
Monitor your bank and credit card statements closely for unfamiliar charges.

title: Car break-ins on Maple Street
description: Multiple cars on Maple Street had their windows broken overnight. Items were stolen from at least three vehicles. A suspicious person was seen walking through the neighborhood around 2 AM. Residents are advised to lock their cars and remove valuables.
summary: This report describes a physical safety concern in the community. Prioritize personal safety and contact local authorities if needed.
checklist: If you are in immediate danger, call 911 or your local emergency number.
Document the incident with photos, timestamps, and descriptions.
File a report with your local police department.
Alert neighbors or community members to increase awareness.

title: Package delivery scam text messages
description: Several neighbors have reported receiving text messages claiming a package could not be delivered and requesting a small payment to reschedule. The link in the text leads to a fake shipping website that asks for credit card information.
summary: This report describes a potential scam or fraud attempt seeking money or sensitive information. Do not engage with the requester.
checklist: Stop all communication with the suspected scammer immediately.
Do not send money, gift cards, or personal information.
Report the scam to the FTC at reportfraud.ftc.gov.
Alert your bank or financial institution if you shared payment details.

title: Ransomware warning from local business network
description: A local small business reported that their computer systems were locked by ransomware. The attackers are demanding payment in cryptocurrency. The business has disconnected from the internet and is working with IT support. Other businesses in the area should be on alert.
summary: This report describes a potential network or cybersecurity threat. Investigate affected systems and secure network access promptly.
checklist: Disconnect the affected device from the network if you suspect active compromise.
Change your Wi-Fi password and any reused passwords immediately.
Run a full antivirus/malware scan on all connected devices.
Check your router firmware for updates and disable remote management.

title: Vandalism at community park
description: The playground equipment at Riverside Community Park was vandalized overnight. Graffiti was sprayed on the slides and two benches were damaged. This is the second incident of property damage this month.
summary: This report describes a physical safety concern in the community. Prioritize personal safety and contact local authorities if needed.
checklist: If you are in immediate danger, call 911 or your local emergency number.
Document the incident with photos, timestamps, and descriptions.
File a report with your local police department.
Alert neighbors or community members to increase awareness.

title: Suspicious email asking for company login credentials
description: Employees at a local co-working space received an email that looks like it came from the building management asking them to log in to a portal to update their access badge. The email has a link to a fake login page that harvests passwords and credentials.
summary: This report describes a possible phishing attempt designed to steal credentials or personal information. Exercise caution and avoid interacting with the suspicious message.
checklist: Do not click any links or download attachments from the suspicious message.
If you already clicked, change your password immediately for the affected account.
Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) on your accounts.
Report the phishing attempt to the impersonated organization via their official website.

title: Elderly neighbor targeted by tech support scam
description: My elderly neighbor received a pop-up on their computer claiming it was infected with a virus and telling them to call a phone number for Microsoft support. They called and the scammer asked for remote access to their computer and a payment of 300 dollars via wire transfer.
summary: This report describes a potential scam or fraud attempt seeking money or sensitive information. Do not engage with the requester.
checklist: Stop all communication with the suspected scammer immediately.
Do not send money, gift cards, or personal information.
Report the scam to the FTC at reportfraud.ftc.gov.
Alert your bank or financial institution if you shared payment details.
